    95‚Üí/-!
    96‚ÜíMaximal Sidon sets in an interval.
    97‚Üí
    98‚ÜíWe follow the convention that `IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N` means `A ‚äÜ {1, ‚Ä¶, N}` is Sidon and
    99‚Üíis inclusion-maximal among subsets of `Set.Icc 1 N` with the Sidon property.
   100‚Üí-/
   101‚Üí
   102‚Üí/-- `IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N` means `A ‚äÜ {1, ‚Ä¶, N}` is Sidon and cannot be extended within
   103‚Üí`{1, ‚Ä¶, N}` while remaining Sidon. -/
   104‚Üídef IsMaximalSidonSetIn (A : Set ‚Ąē) (N : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
   105‚Üí  A ‚äÜ Set.Icc 1 N ‚ąß IsSidon A ‚ąß
   106‚Üí    ‚ąÄ ‚¶Éx : ‚Ąē‚¶Ą, x ‚ąą Set.Icc 1 N ‚Üí x ‚ąČ A ‚Üí ¬¨ IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {x})
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üínamespace IsMaximalSidonSetIn
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí/-- If `A` is a maximal Sidon set in `{1, ‚Ä¶, N}`, then `A ‚äÜ {1, ‚Ä¶, N}`. -/
   111‚Üítheorem subset {A : Set ‚Ąē} {N : ‚Ąē} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) :
   112‚Üí    A ‚äÜ Set.Icc 1 N := hA.1
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí/-- If `A` is a maximal Sidon set in `{1, ‚Ä¶, N}`, then `A` is Sidon. -/
   115‚Üítheorem isSidon {A : Set ‚Ąē} {N : ‚Ąē} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) : IsSidon A := hA.2.1
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí/-- Maximality condition unpacked. -/
   118‚Üítheorem maximal {A : Set ‚Ąē} {N : ‚Ąē} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) {x : ‚Ąē}
   119‚Üí    (hx : x ‚ąą Set.Icc 1 N) (hxA : x ‚ąČ A) : ¬¨ IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {x}) := hA.2.2 hx hxA
   120‚Üí

   127‚Üíinstance (A : Finset őĪ) [DecidableEq őĪ] : Decidable (IsSidon (A : Set őĪ)) :=
   128‚Üí  decidable_of_iff (‚ąÄŠĶČ (i‚āĀ ‚ąą A) (j‚āĀ ‚ąą A) (i‚āā ‚ąą A) (j‚āā ‚ąą A), _) <| by rfl
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üí/-- The maximum size of a Sidon set in the supplied `Finset`. -/
   131‚Üídef maxSidonSubsetCard (A : Finset őĪ) [DecidableEq őĪ] : ‚Ąē :=
   132‚Üí  (A.powerset.filter fun B : Finset őĪ ‚Ü¶ IsSidon (B : Set őĪ)).sup Finset.card
   133‚Üí
   134‚Üí/-- If `A` is finite Sidon, then `A ‚ą™ {s}` is also Sidon provided `s ‚Č• A.max + 1`. -/
   135‚Üítheorem IsSidon.insert_ge_max' {A : Finset ‚Ąē} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set ‚Ąē)) {s : ‚Ąē}
   136‚Üí    (hs : 2 * A.max' h + 1 ‚Č§ s) :
   137‚Üí    IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {s}) := by
   138‚Üí  have h‚āĀ {a b c : ‚Ąē} (ha : a ‚ąą A) (hb : b ‚ąą A) (hc : c ‚ąą A) :
   139‚Üí        a + b < 2 * A.max' h + 1 + c := by linarith [A.le_max' _ ha, A.le_max' _ hb]
   140‚Üí  have : s ‚ąČ A := by
   141‚Üí    exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ |>.2 fun a ha ‚Ü¶ by
   142‚Üí      linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   143‚Üí  exact (IsSidon.insert hA).2 <| by simpa [this] using fun a ha b hb ‚Ü¶
   144‚Üí    ‚ü®by linarith [A.le_max' _ ha, A.le_max' _ hb], fun c hc ‚Ü¶ by linarith [h‚āĀ hc hb ha]‚ü©
   145‚Üí
   146‚Üítheorem IsSidon.exists_insert {A : Finset ‚Ąē} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set ‚Ąē)) :
   147‚Üí    ‚ąÉ m ‚ąČ A, IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {m}) := by
   148‚Üí  refine ‚ü®2 * A.max' h + 1, ?_, insert_ge_max' h hA le_rfl‚ü©
   149‚Üí  exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ |>.2 fun a ha ‚Ü¶ by
   150‚Üí    linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üítheorem IsSidon.exists_insert_ge {A : Finset ‚Ąē} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set ‚Ąē)) (s : ‚Ąē) :
   153‚Üí    ‚ąÉ m ‚Č• s, m ‚ąČ A ‚ąß IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {m}) := by
   154‚Üí  refine ‚ü®if s ‚Č• 2 * A.max' h + 1 then s else 2 * A.max' h + 1, ?_, ?_, ?_‚ü©
   155‚Üí  ¬∑ split_ifs <;> linarith
   156‚Üí  ¬∑ split_ifs <;>
   157‚Üí    exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ |>.2 fun a ha ‚Ü¶ by
   158‚Üí      linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   159‚Üí  ¬∑ split_ifs with hs
   160‚Üí    ¬∑ exact insert_ge_max' h hA hs
   161‚Üí    ¬∑ exact insert_ge_max' h hA le_rfl

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Schur's theorem on Galois groups of truncated exponential polynomials
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* (https://math.stackexchange.com/questions/2814220)
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí*Reference* (https://mathoverflow.net/questions/477077)
    25‚Üí-/
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí/-
    28‚ÜíNote: This was asked by Nick Katz. Quasi-autoformalized using Claude 4.0 Sonnet.
    29‚Üí-/
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üínamespace SchurTruncatedExponential
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üíopen Polynomial
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üíopen scoped Nat
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/--
    38‚ÜíThe truncated exponential polynomial `truncatedExp n` is
    39‚Üígiven by `‚ąĎ_{j=0}^{n} x^j / j!` over `‚Ąö`, which is the
    40‚Üí`n`-th partial sum of the Taylor series for the exponential function `e^x`.
    41‚Üí-/
    42‚Üínoncomputable def truncatedExp (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąö[X] :=
    43‚Üí  ‚ąĎ j ‚ąą Finset.range (n + 1), (1 / j ! : ‚Ąö) ‚ÄĘ X ^ j
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/--
    46‚Üí**Schur's Theorem (1924):**
    47‚ÜíLet `f_n(x) = ‚ąĎ_{j=0}^n x^j/j!` be the `n`-th truncated
    48‚Üíexponential polynomial over `‚Ąö`. Then for `n ‚Č• 2`:
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí- If `n ‚Č° 0 (mod 4)`, the Galois group of `f_n` is isomorphic to the alternating group `A_n`
    51‚Üí- If `n ‚ČĘ 0 (mod 4)`, the Galois group of `f_n` is isomorphic to the symmetric group `S_n`
    52‚Üí-/
    53‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 12]
    54‚Üítheorem schur_truncatedExp_galoisGroup_equiv (n : ‚Ąē) (hn : n ‚Č• 2) :
    55‚Üí  letI f := truncatedExp n
    56‚Üí  if n % 4 = 0 then
    57‚Üí    -- Galois group is alternating group A_n
    58‚Üí    Nonempty (f.Gal ‚ČÉ* alternatingGroup (Fin n))
    59‚Üí  else
    60‚Üí    -- Galois group is symmetric group S_n
    61‚Üí    Nonempty (f.Gal ‚ČÉ* Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) := by
    62‚Üí  sorry
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üíend SchurTruncatedExponential

    27‚Üí/--
    28‚ÜíA set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is sum-free in the sense of ErdŇĎs if no element of $A$ can be
    29‚Üíexpressed as the sum of finitely many distinct smaller elements of $A$.

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 877
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/877](https://www.erdosproblems.com/877)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $f_m(n)$ count the number of maximal sum-free subsets $A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, n\}$ ‚ÄĒ that is,
    25‚Üí$A$ contains no three elements $a, b, c$ with $a = b + c$, and $A$ is maximal with this
    26‚Üíproperty (no element of $\{1, \ldots, n\} \setminus A$ can be added while preserving sum-freeness).
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíIs it true that $f_m(n) = o(2^{n/2})$?
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíCameron and ErdŇĎs [CaEr90] proved that $f_m(n) > 2^{n/4}$. Luczak and Schoen [LuSc01] proved
    31‚Üíthat there exists $c < 1/2$ with $f_m(n) < 2^{cn}$, resolving the conjecture.
    32‚ÜíBalogh, Liu, Sharifzadeh, and Treglown [BLST15] proved $f_m(n) = 2^{(1/4+o(1))n}$,
    33‚Üílater refined [BLST18] to $f_m(n) = (C_n + o(1)) \cdot 2^{n/4}$.
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíopen scoped Classical
    37‚Üíopen Finset
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üínamespace Erdos877
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/-- A finite set of natural numbers is *sum-free* if for all $b, c \in A$,
    42‚Üíwe have $b + c \notin A$. -/
    43‚Üídef IsSumFree (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    44‚Üí  ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ c ‚ąą A, b + c ‚ąČ A
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/-- A subset $A$ of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$ is a *maximal sum-free subset* if it is sum-free
    47‚Üíand no element of $\{1, \ldots, n\} \setminus A$ can be added while preserving
    48‚Üísum-freeness. -/
    49‚Üídef IsMaximalSumFree (A : Finset ‚Ąē) (n : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    50‚Üí  A ‚äÜ Finset.Icc 1 n ‚ąß
    51‚Üí  IsSumFree A ‚ąß
    52‚Üí  ‚ąÄ x ‚ąą Finset.Icc 1 n, x ‚ąČ A ‚Üí ¬¨ IsSumFree (insert x A)
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/-- The number of maximal sum-free subsets of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$. -/
    55‚Üínoncomputable def maximalSumFreeCount (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    56‚Üí  ((Finset.Icc 1 n).powerset.filter (fun A => IsMaximalSumFree A n)).card
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí/--
    59‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 877 [CaEr90][Er98]:
    60‚Üí
    61‚ÜíLet $f_m(n)$ count the number of maximal sum-free subsets of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$. Then
    62‚Üí$$f_m(n) = o(2^{n/2}).$$
    63‚Üí
    64‚ÜíThat is, for every $\varepsilon > 0$, for all sufficiently large $n$,
    65‚Üí$$f_m(n) \leq \varepsilon \cdot 2^{n/2}.$$
    66‚Üí
    67‚ÜíProved by Luczak and Schoen [LuSc01], with the sharp asymptotics
    68‚Üí$f_m(n) = 2^{(1/4+o(1))n}$ established by Balogh‚ÄďLiu‚ÄďSharifzadeh‚ÄďTreglown [BLST15].
    69‚Üí-/
    70‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    71‚Üítheorem erdos_877 :
    72‚Üí    answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    73‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    74‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    75‚Üí      (maximalSumFreeCount n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ őĶ * (2 : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((n : ‚ĄĚ) / 2) := by
    76‚Üí  sorry
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üíend Erdos877

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 867
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/867](https://www.erdosproblems.com/867)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíErdŇĎs asked whether a consecutive-sum-free subset of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ can have at most
    25‚Üí$N/2 + C$ elements for some absolute constant $C$. This was disproved by Coppersmith and Phillips.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Er92c] ErdŇĎs, P., _Some of my favourite problems which recently have been solved_. Proceedings
    28‚Üíof the international conference on set-theoretic topology and its applications, Part 2, Matsuyama
    29‚Üí(1992).
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[CoPh96] Coppersmith, D. and Phillips, S., _Sums along the rationals_. Preprint (1996).
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen Finset
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos867
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- A finite set $A$ of natural numbers is "consecutive-sum-free" if, when its
    39‚Üíelements are listed in increasing order as $a_1 < a_2 < \cdots < a_t$, no sum of
    40‚Üítwo or more consecutive elements ($a_i + a_{i+1} + \cdots + a_j$ with $i < j$)
    41‚Üílies in $A$. -/
    42‚Üídef ConsecutiveSumFree (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    43‚Üí  let s := A.sort (¬∑ ‚Č§ ¬∑)
    44‚Üí  ‚ąÄ i j : ‚Ąē, i < j ‚Üí j < s.length ‚Üí
    45‚Üí    (‚ąĎ k ‚ąą Finset.Icc i j, s.getD k 0) ‚ąČ A
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/--
    48‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 867 (disproved) [Er92c]:
    49‚Üí
    50‚ÜíIs it true that if $A = \{a_1 < \cdots < a_t\} \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ has no sum of two or
    51‚Üímore consecutive elements in $A$, then $|A| \le N/2 + C$ for some absolute constant $C$?
    52‚Üí
    53‚ÜíDisproved by Coppersmith and Phillips [CoPh96], who proved the maximum $|A|$ satisfies
    54‚Üí$$
    55‚Üí  \frac{13}{24} N - O(1) \le |A| \le \left(\frac{2}{3} - \frac{1}{512}\right) N + \log N.
    56‚Üí$$
    57‚Üí-/
    58‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    59‚Üítheorem erdos_867 : answer(False) ‚ÜĒ
    60‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ A : Finset ‚Ąē, A ‚äÜ Finset.Icc 1 N ‚Üí
    61‚Üí    ConsecutiveSumFree A ‚Üí
    62‚Üí    A.card ‚Č§ N / 2 + C := by
    63‚Üí  sorry
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üíend Erdos867
    66‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 792
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/792](https://www.erdosproblems.com/792)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $f(n)$ be maximal such that in any $A \subset \mathbb{Z}$ with $|A| = n$ there exists some
    25‚Üísum-free subset $B \subseteq A$ with $|B| \geq f(n)$, so that there are no solutions to
    26‚Üí$a + b = c$ with $a, b, c \in B$. Estimate $f(n)$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíErdŇĎs [Er65] gave a simple proof that $f(n) \geq n/3$. The best lower bound known is
    29‚Üí$f(n) \geq n/3 + c \log \log n$ for some $c > 0$, due to Bedert [Be25b]. The best upper
    30‚Üíbound known is $f(n) \leq n/3 + o(n)$, due to Eberhard, Green, and Manners [EGM14].
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí[Er65] ErdŇĎs, P., *Extremal problems in number theory*. Proc. Sympos. Pure Math.,
    33‚ÜíVol. VIII (1965), 181-189.
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[Be25b] Bedert, B., *On the size of sum-free subsets* (2025).
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[EGM14] Eberhard, S., Green, B., and Manners, F., *Sets of integers with no large
    38‚Üísum-free subset*. Ann. of Math. (2) 180 (2014), 621-652.
    39‚Üí-/
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üínamespace Erdos792
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/-- A finset of integers is sum-free if for all $a, b$ in the set, $a + b$ is not
    44‚Üíin the set. -/
    45‚Üídef IsSumFreeSet (B : Finset ‚Ą§) : Prop :=
    46‚Üí  ‚ąÄ a ‚ąą B, ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą B, a + b ‚ąČ B
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- $f(n)$ is the largest $k$ such that every $n$-element subset of $\mathbb{Z}$ contains
    49‚Üía sum-free subset of size at least $k$. -/
    50‚Üínoncomputable def maxSumFreeSize (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    51‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÄ (A : Finset ‚Ą§), A.card = n ‚Üí
    52‚Üí    ‚ąÉ B : Finset ‚Ą§, B ‚äÜ A ‚ąß IsSumFreeSet B ‚ąß k ‚Č§ B.card}
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/--
    55‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 792, ErdŇĎs's lower bound [Er65]:
    56‚Üí$f(n) \geq n/3$ for all $n$.
    57‚Üí-/
    58‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    59‚Üítheorem erdos_792 (n : ‚Ąē) :
    60‚Üí    (maxSumFreeSize n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• n / 3 := by
    61‚Üí  sorry
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/--
    64‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 792, best known lower bound (Bedert [Be25b]):
    65‚ÜíThere exists $c > 0$ such that $f(n) \geq n/3 + c \cdot \log(\log(n))$ for all
    66‚Üísufficiently large $n$.
    67‚Üí-/
    68‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    69‚Üítheorem erdos_792.variants.lower_bedert :
    70‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, c > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    71‚Üí      (maxSumFreeSize n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• n / 3 + c * Real.log (Real.log n) := by
    72‚Üí  sorry
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí/--
    75‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 792, best known upper bound (Eberhard‚ÄďGreen‚ÄďManners [EGM14]):
    76‚Üí$f(n) \leq n/3 + o(n)$, i.e., for every $\varepsilon > 0$,
    77‚Üí$f(n) \leq (1/3 + \varepsilon) \cdot n$ for all sufficiently large $n$.
    78‚Üí-/
    79‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    80‚Üítheorem erdos_792.variants.upper_egm :
    81‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    82‚Üí      (maxSumFreeSize n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ (1 / 3 + őĶ) * n := by
    83‚Üí  sorry
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üíend Erdos792
    86‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 790
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/790](https://www.erdosproblems.com/790)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $l(n)$ be maximal such that if $A \subset \mathbb{Z}$ with $|A| = n$ then there exists a
    25‚Üísum-free $B \subseteq A$ with $|B| \geq l(n)$ ‚ÄĒ that is, $B$ is such that there are no
    26‚Üísolutions to $a_1 = a_2 + \cdots + a_r$ with $a_i \in B$ all distinct.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíChoi, Koml√≥s, and Szemer√©di [CKS75] proved
    29‚Üí$$
    30‚Üí(n \log n / \log \log n)^{1/2} \ll l(n) \ll n / \log n.
    31‚Üí$$
    32‚ÜíThey further conjecture that $l(n) \geq n^{1 - o(1)}$.
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí[CKS75] Choi, S. L. G., Koml√≥s, J., and Szemer√©di, E., _On sum-free subsequences_,
    35‚ÜíTrans. Amer. Math. Soc. 212 (1975), 307‚Äď313.
    36‚Üí-/
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace Erdos790
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- A finset $B \subseteq \mathbb{Z}$ is "sum-free" in the sense of ErdŇĎs Problem 790 if no
    41‚Üíelement of $B$ equals the sum of two or more other distinct elements of $B$. -/
    42‚Üídef IsSumFree (B : Finset ‚Ą§) : Prop :=
    43‚Üí  ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą B, ‚ąÄ S : Finset ‚Ą§, S ‚äÜ B.erase b ‚Üí S.card ‚Č• 2 ‚Üí b ‚Č† S.sum id
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- $l(n)$: the largest $l$ such that every $n$-element subset $A$ of $\mathbb{Z}$ contains a
    46‚Üísum-free subset $B$ with $|B| \geq l$. -/
    47‚Üínoncomputable def maxSumFreeSize (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    48‚Üí  sSup {l : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÄ (A : Finset ‚Ą§), A.card = n ‚Üí
    49‚Üí    ‚ąÉ B : Finset ‚Ą§, B ‚äÜ A ‚ąß l ‚Č§ B.card ‚ąß IsSumFree B}
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/--
    52‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 790** ‚ÄĒ Is it true that $l(n) n^{-1/2} \to \infty$? That is, for every $C > 0$
    53‚Üíthere exists $N_0$ such that $l(n) \geq C \cdot n^{1/2}$ for all $n \geq N_0$.
    54‚Üí-/
    55‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    56‚Üítheorem erdos_790 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    57‚Üí    ‚ąÄ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚Üí ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    58‚Üí      (maxSumFreeSize n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• C * (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((1 : ‚ĄĚ) / 2) := by
    59‚Üí  sorry
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí/--
    62‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 790** ‚ÄĒ Is it true that $l(n) < n^{1-c}$ for some $c > 0$? That is, there
    63‚Üíexist $c > 0$ and $N_0$ such that $l(n) \leq n^{1-c}$ for all $n \geq N_0$.
    64‚Üí-/
    65‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    66‚Üítheorem erdos_790.variants.upper_bound_conjecture : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    67‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, c > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    68‚Üí      (maxSumFreeSize n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ (1 - c) := by
    69‚Üí  sorry
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/--
    72‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 790** ‚ÄĒ CKS lower bound [CKS75]:
    73‚Üí$$
    74‚Üíl(n) \gg \left(\frac{n \log n}{\log \log n}\right)^{1/2}.
    75‚Üí$$
    76‚Üí-/
    77‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    78‚Üítheorem erdos_790.variants.cks_lower :
    79‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    80‚Üí      (maxSumFreeSize n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č•
    81‚Üí        C * ((n : ‚ĄĚ) * Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.log (Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ))) ^ ((1 : ‚ĄĚ) / 2) := by
    82‚Üí  sorry
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí/--
    85‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 790** ‚ÄĒ CKS upper bound [CKS75]:
    86‚Üí$$
    87‚Üíl(n) \ll \frac{n}{\log n}.
    88‚Üí$$
    89‚Üí-/
    90‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    91‚Üítheorem erdos_790.variants.cks_upper :
    92‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    93‚Üí      (maxSumFreeSize n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ C * ((n : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
    94‚Üí  sorry
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí/--
    97‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 790** ‚ÄĒ CKS conjecture [CKS75]: $l(n) \geq n^{1 - o(1)}$, i.e., for every
    98‚Üí$\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $N_0$ such that $l(n) \geq n^{1 - \varepsilon}$ for all
    99‚Üí$n \geq N_0$.
   100‚Üí-/
   101‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
   102‚Üítheorem erdos_790.variants.cks_conjecture :
   103‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
   104‚Üí      (maxSumFreeSize n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ (1 - őĶ) := by
   105‚Üí  sorry
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üíend Erdos790

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 748
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/748](https://www.erdosproblems.com/748)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíThe Cameron-ErdŇĎs Conjecture. Let $f(n)$ count the number of sum-free subsets
    25‚Üí$A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, n\}$, i.e., $A$ contains no three elements $a$, $b$, $c$ with
    26‚Üí$a = b + c$. Is it true that $f(n) = 2^{(1+o(1)) n/2}$?
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíThe lower bound $f(n) \geq 2^{n/2}$ is trivial (consider all subsets of
    29‚Üí$\{\lceil n/2 \rceil, \ldots, n\}$).
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíThis was proved independently by Green [Gr04] and Sapozhenko [Sa03], who showed
    32‚Üí$f(n) \sim c_n \cdot 2^{n/2}$ where $c_n$ depends on the parity of $n$.
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí[CaEr90] Cameron, P.J. and ErdŇĎs, P.
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí[Er94b] ErdŇĎs, P.
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí[Er98] ErdŇĎs, P.
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí[Gr04] Green, B., _The Cameron-ErdŇĎs conjecture_, Bull. London Math. Soc. 36 (2004), 769-778.
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí[Sa03] Sapozhenko, A.A., _The Cameron-ErdŇĎs conjecture_, Discrete Math. 235 (2003), 131-138.
    43‚Üí-/
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üíopen Finset
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üínamespace Erdos748
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- A finite set of natural numbers is *sum-free* if it contains no three
    50‚Üí    elements $a$, $b$, $c$ (not necessarily distinct) with $b + c = a$. Equivalently,
    51‚Üí    for all $b, c \in A$, we have $b + c \notin A$. -/
    52‚Üídef IsSumFree (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    53‚Üí  ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ c ‚ąą A, b + c ‚ąČ A
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üíinstance : DecidablePred IsSumFree :=
    56‚Üí  fun A => decidable_of_iff (‚ąÄ b ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ c ‚ąą A, b + c ‚ąČ A) Iff.rfl
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí/-- The number of sum-free subsets of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$. -/
    59‚Üínoncomputable def sumFreeSubsetCount (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    60‚Üí  ((Finset.Icc 1 n).powerset.filter (fun A => IsSumFree A)).card
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/--
    63‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 748 (Cameron-ErdŇĎs Conjecture) [CaEr90][Er94b][Er98]:
    64‚Üí
    65‚ÜíLet $f(n)$ count the number of sum-free subsets of $\{1, \ldots, n\}$. Then
    66‚Üí$$f(n) = 2^{(1+o(1)) n/2}.$$
    67‚Üí
    68‚ÜíEquivalently: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, for all sufficiently large $n$,
    69‚Üí$$2^{(1 - \varepsilon) \cdot n/2} \leq f(n) \leq 2^{(1 + \varepsilon) \cdot n/2}.$$
    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíProved independently by Green [Gr04] and Sapozhenko [Sa03].
    72‚Üí-/
    73‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    74‚Üítheorem erdos_748 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    75‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    76‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    77‚Üí      (2 : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((1 - őĶ) * (n : ‚ĄĚ) / 2) ‚Č§ (sumFreeSubsetCount n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚ąß

    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí[Er61] ErdŇĎs, P., _Some unsolved problems_, Magyar Tud. Akad. Mat. Kutat√≥ Int. K√∂zl. 6 (1961),

    30‚Üí
    31‚Üínamespace Erdos483
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí/-- A coloring $f : \mathbb{N} \to \text{Fin}\ k$ has a *monochromatic Schur triple* in
    34‚Üí$\{1, \ldots, N\}$ if there exist $a, b \geq 1$ with $a + b \leq N$ and
    35‚Üí$f(a) = f(b) = f(a + b)$. -/
    36‚Üídef HasMonochromaticSchurTriple (k N : ‚Ąē) (f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí Fin k) : Prop :=
    37‚Üí  ‚ąÉ a b : ‚Ąē, 1 ‚Č§ a ‚ąß 1 ‚Č§ b ‚ąß a + b ‚Č§ N ‚ąß f a = f b ‚ąß f b = f (a + b)
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/--
    40‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 483 [Er61, p.233] [Er65, p.188]:
    41‚Üí
    42‚ÜíLet $f(k)$ be the minimal $N$ such that if $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ is $k$-coloured then there is a
    43‚Üímonochromatic solution to $a + b = c$. The values $f(k)$ are known as Schur numbers.
    44‚Üí
    45‚ÜíEstimate $f(k)$. In particular, is it true that $f(k) < c^k$ for some constant $c > 0$?
    46‚Üí
    47‚ÜíThe best-known bounds for large $k$ are
    48‚Üí$$
    49‚Üí  380^{k/5} - O(1) \leq f(k) \leq \lfloor (e - 1/24)\, k! \rfloor - 1.
    50‚Üí$$
    51‚ÜíThe known values are $f(1) = 2$, $f(2) = 5$, $f(3) = 14$, $f(4) = 45$, $f(5) = 161$
    52‚Üí(OEIS A030126).
    53‚Üí
    54‚ÜíWe formalize the conjecture: there exists $c > 0$ such that for all $k \geq 1$,
    55‚Üíevery $k$-coloring of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ with $N \geq c^k$ has a monochromatic Schur triple.
    56‚ÜíEquivalently, the Schur number $f(k)$ grows at most exponentially in $k$.
    57‚Üí-/

    61‚Üí      ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, 1 ‚Č§ k ‚Üí
    62‚Üí        ‚ąÄ N : ‚Ąē, c ^ k ‚Č§ (N : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí

    64‚Üí            HasMonochromaticSchurTriple k N f := by

    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/535](https://www.erdosproblems.com/535)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $r \geq 3$, and let $f_r(N)$ denote the size of the largest subset of
    25‚Üí$\{1, \ldots, N\}$ such that no subset of size $r$ has the same pairwise
    26‚Üígreatest common divisor between all elements. Estimate $f_r(N)$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíErdŇĎs [Er64] proved that $f_r(N) \leq N^{3/4+o(1)}$, and Abbott and Hanson
    29‚Üí[AbHa70] improved this exponent to $1/2$. ErdŇĎs [Er64] proved the lower bound
    30‚Üí$f_3(N) > N^{c/\log\log N}$ for some constant $c > 0$, and conjectured this
    31‚Üíshould also be an upper bound.
    32‚Üí
    33‚ÜíThis problem is intimately connected with the sunflower problem [20].
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[Er64] ErdŇĎs, P., *On extremal problems of graphs and generalized graphs*,
    36‚ÜíIsrael J. Math. 2 (1964), 183--190.
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí[AbHa70] Abbott, H.L. and Hanson, D., *A problem of Schur and its
    39‚Üígeneralizations*, Acta Arith. 20 (1970), 175--187.

    44‚Üínamespace Erdos535
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/-- A set $A$ of natural numbers is $r$-GCD-uniform-free if no $r$-element
    47‚Üísubset has all pairwise GCDs equal. That is, there is no $r$-element subset
    48‚Üí$S \subseteq A$ and value $d$ such that $\gcd(a, b) = d$ for all distinct
    49‚Üí$a, b \in S$. -/
    50‚Üídef IsGCDUniformFree (A : Finset ‚Ąē) (r : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    51‚Üí  ‚ąÄ S : Finset ‚Ąē, S ‚äÜ A ‚Üí S.card = r ‚Üí

    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/--
    55‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 535** (Upper bound conjecture):
    56‚Üí
    57‚ÜíFor each $r \geq 3$, there exists a constant $c > 0$ and $N_0$ such that for all
    58‚Üí$N \geq N_0$, every $r$-GCD-uniform-free subset of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ has size
    59‚Üíat most $N^{c / \log\log N}$.

    61‚ÜíSee [Er64].

    65‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < c ‚ąß
    66‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ N : ‚Ąē, N‚āÄ ‚Č§ N ‚Üí
    67‚Üí      ‚ąÄ A : Finset ‚Ąē, A ‚äÜ Icc 1 N ‚Üí IsGCDUniformFree A r ‚Üí
    68‚Üí        (A.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ (N : ‚ĄĚ) ^ (c / Real.log (Real.log (N : ‚ĄĚ))) := by

    73‚Üí

    75‚Üíthere exists a $3$-GCD-uniform-free subset of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ of size at
    76‚Üíleast $N^{c / \log\log N}$.

    78‚ÜíSee [Er64].

    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs [Er76d][Er79d]. The Sylvester-Schur theorem states that

    24‚ÜíIf $A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ satisfies $\sum_{a \in A} 1/a > \delta \cdot \log N$ for some
    25‚Üí$\delta > 0$ and $N$ sufficiently large, then $A$ contains a subset whose reciprocals sum to $1$.
    26‚Üí

    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 37
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/37](https://www.erdosproblems.com/37)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA lacunary set cannot be an essential component (a set that strictly increases the
    25‚ÜíSchnirelmann density of every set it is added to). Proved by Ruzsa [Ru87].
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Ru87] Ruzsa, I.Z., _Essential components_. Proc. London Math. Soc. (3) **54** (1987), 38‚Äď56.
    28‚Üí-/
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üíopen Classical
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üínamespace Erdos37
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí/--
    35‚ÜíThe Schnirelmann density of a set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$, defined as
    36‚Üí$$d_s(A) = \inf_{n \geq 1} \frac{|A \cap \{1,\ldots,n\}|}{n}$$
    37‚Üí-/
    38‚Üínoncomputable def schnirelmannDensity (A : Set ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    39‚Üí  ‚®Ö n : ‚Ąē+, (((Finset.Icc 1 (n : ‚Ąē)).filter (¬∑ ‚ąą A)).card : ‚ĄĚ) / ((n : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ)
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/--
    42‚ÜíThe sumset $A + B = \{a + b \mid a \in A, b \in B\}$ for sets of natural numbers.
    43‚Üí-/
    44‚Üídef sumset (A B : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    45‚Üí  {n | ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą B, n = a + b}
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/--
    48‚ÜíA set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an *essential component* if $d_s(A + B) > d_s(B)$ for every
    49‚Üí$B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ with $0 < d_s(B) < 1$, where $d_s$ is the Schnirelmann density.
    50‚Üí-/
    51‚Üídef IsEssentialComponent (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    52‚Üí  ‚ąÄ (B : Set ‚Ąē), 0 < schnirelmannDensity B ‚Üí schnirelmannDensity B < 1 ‚Üí
    53‚Üí    schnirelmannDensity (sumset A B) > schnirelmannDensity B
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/--
    56‚ÜíA set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is *lacunary* if there exists $q > 1$ such that for any two
    57‚Üíconsecutive elements $a < b$ of $A$ (with no element of $A$ strictly between them),
    58‚Üíwe have $b \geq q \cdot a$.
    59‚Üí-/
    60‚Üídef IsLacunary (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    61‚Üí  ‚ąÉ (q : ‚ĄĚ), q > 1 ‚ąß ‚ąÄ (a b : ‚Ąē), a ‚ąą A ‚Üí b ‚ąą A ‚Üí a < b ‚Üí
    62‚Üí    (‚ąÄ c ‚ąą A, ¬¨(a < c ‚ąß c < b)) ‚Üí (b : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• q * (a : ‚ĄĚ)
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 37, proved by Ruzsa [Ru87].
    66‚ÜíA lacunary set cannot be an essential component.
    67‚ÜíRuzsa proved that if $A$ is an essential component then there exists some constant
    68‚Üí$c > 0$ such that $|A \cap \{1,\ldots,N\}| \geq (\log N)^{1+c}$ for all large $N$, which rules
    69‚Üíout lacunary sets (which satisfy $|A \cap \{1,\ldots,N\}| = O(\log N)$).
    70‚Üí-/
    71‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    72‚Üítheorem erdos_37 :
    73‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (A : Set ‚Ąē), IsLacunary A ‚Üí ¬¨IsEssentialComponent A := by

    76‚Üíend Erdos37
